title: Spectrophotometric determination of molybdenum(VI) using salicylaldehyde 3-oxobutanoylhydrazone as a new analytical reagent
author: A. K. Blshnoi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Industrial Chemistry, Guru Nanak Khalsa College, Yamuna Nagar-135 001, Haryana, India 

  E-mail  : anilkbishnoi @rediffmail.com 

 Department of Chemistry, N. A. S. College, Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, India 

  Manuscript received 7 January 2005, revised 29 May 2006, accepted 6 June 2006  

 Salicylaldehyde 3-oxobutanoylhydrazone (salicylaldehyde acetoacetic acid hydrazone, SAAR) has been use as an analytical reagent for the spectrophotometric determination of molybdenum(VI),The metal ion formed a 1 : 1 greenish-yellow complex with the reagent having absorption maximum at 405 nm,which was quantitatively extracted Into benzene.Under the optimum conditions of the proposed procedure Beer's law was obeyed over the concentration range of 0-18.7 μg Mo mr -1  The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of the method were found to be 9.96 x 10 3  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.0096 μg Mo cm -2  with a relative standard deviation of ±0.0025 absorbance units and coefficient of variance as 0.25% respectively.The proposed method has been applied successfully for micro determination of molybdenum In various alloy steel and a wide variety of synthetic and technical samples with satisfactory results.
